title: Confirmation depth tracking
description:
What: Track confirmation depth for in-flight incoming payments and fire ConfirmationEvent when depth >= MIN_CONFIRMATIONS.

Note: This logic lives INSIDE the nie-ghf watcher task (same file/module). It is a distinct concern (depth tracking state) separated from the block scanning loop.

Algorithm (state managed by watcher task):
1. Maintain pending: HashMap<String, (Uuid, u64)> mapping txid -> (session_id, found_at_height).
2. When nie-ghf detects a hit (address in registry): insert into pending (skip if already present — dedup).
3. On each new block (current_height): for each entry in pending:
   - depth = current_height - found_at_height.
   - If depth >= MIN_CONFIRMATIONS: send ConfirmationEvent { session_id, txid, block_height: found_at_height } to conf_tx, remove from pending.
4. MIN_CONFIRMATIONS: u64 = 10 defined as a named const at module scope.

Defensive programming:
- found_at_height must be <= current_height; if somehow current < found (reorg?), skip silently and log warn.
- pending is local to the watcher task (not shared); no locking needed.
- Never log session_id alongside key material.

Tests:
- test_depth_fires_at_threshold: insert entry at height 100, advance to height 109 (depth 9, no event), height 110 (depth 10, event fires). Oracle: depth formula = current - found, threshold = 10 by spec.
- test_depth_no_duplicate_fire: once event fires, entry removed; advancing more blocks does not re-fire.
- test_min_confirmations_const: assert MIN_CONFIRMATIONS == 10 (spec-derived constant, not from code under test).

Acceptance: cargo test passes for depth tests; MIN_CONFIRMATIONS == 10 asserted; clippy clean.
close_reason: Implemented in spawn_block_watcher() in cli/src/commands.rs. MIN_CONFIRMATIONS=10 const, pending HashMap depth tracking, ConfirmationEvent firing, exponential backoff, registry-based address matching. All watcher_tests pass.
